name: Factors & Multiples

hooks.visual: Every number has a secret family tree — who are the factors of 24?
hooks.question: Why can you arrange 12 chairs into a perfect rectangle in many ways, but 13 chairs only in one way?

realWorldContext: Factors and multiples help us organize things into equal groups: arranging desks in a classroom, splitting teams evenly, scheduling events that repeat, or figuring out how to share items fairly. They are also the foundation of prime numbers and cryptography.
reflectionPrompt: Why do some numbers have many factors while others have very few?
